title: Living-world route differentiation phase
summary: Lane 4 should make one existing outing materially more tactile in play by letting support choice plus living-world state change how the player finds, reads, or approaches a clue in a lighter-weight biome band.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the first living-world route-differentiation handoff.
execution_order.why_now: The strongest next win is a route that feels different in play, not just in text.

execution_order.goal: Implement the first living-world route-differentiation proof.
execution_order.why_now: One strong tactile proof in a lighter-weight biome band will validate the next direction.

execution_order.goal: Review the first living-world route-differentiation proof.
execution_order.why_now: The first proof should feel materially better without growing another system.

execution_order.goal: Make the first living-world route proof player-felt in live hand-lens play.
execution_order.why_now: The first proof is directionally right, but it still needs a stronger support-shaped live difference before the packet can move on.

execution_order.goal: Review the tightened hand-lens live-difference follow-on.
execution_order.why_now: The packet should only continue once the first proof is genuinely felt in play.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the second living-world route-differentiation handoff.
execution_order.why_now: After one proof lands cleanly, the next step should either extend it to a second light band or simplify a weak support.

execution_order.goal: Implement the second living-world route-differentiation proof or simplification.
execution_order.why_now: A second route or a sharper support seam will prove the direction is reusable.

execution_order.goal: Review the second living-world route-differentiation proof or simplification.
execution_order.why_now: The lane should only expand the pattern if it still reads cleanly and tactily.

guardrails: No new station page, helper row, or route HUD.
Do not answer this pass with more notebook density.
Keep route identity stable while the live moment changes.

main_289_focus.desired_feel: During the live Thaw Window state, support choice plus world-state should change which in-world carrier or approach matters enough that the player plays the route differently, not just reads a different strip.
preferred_shape: reuse the existing process-backed route seam
prefer same-stage, same-band differentiation over a new route branch
prefer truthful alternate carriers that the existing hand-lens targeting can naturally prioritize over more copy

main_290_focus.goal: Prove the slot-local hand-lens preference seam reuses cleanly on Held Sand by preferring the live beach-grass alternate over nearer non-fit back-dune inspectables, while non-hand-lens supports do not.
preferred_shape: reuse the already-authored Held Sand alternate carrier instead of adding a new one
prove the behavior with one hand-lens versus non-hand-lens live back-dune comparison
only touch route logic if the current generic preference fails the end-to-end coastal proof
main_290_focus.probe_note: A late coastal-scrub revisit already yields a back-dune shelf where beach-grass is in range while nearer non-fit entries such as beach-strawberry, beach-pea, or sand-verbena are also nearby.
avoid: broader coastal geometry or content-density edits

main_291_focus.goal: Keep the same alternate `woolly-lousewort` carrier, but make hand-lens produce a player-felt live-difference instead of only a broader acceptance rule.
preferred_shape: tighten the existing hand-lens targeting seam instead of adding a new support or planner surface
scope the preference to the current next slot and active process window only
prove the behavior with one runtime comparison where hand-lens changes the inspected clue and a non-hand-lens support does not
avoid: broad notebook-fit heuristics outside the active route slot
